To address this, we propose a personalized FL framework featuring: (i) a strict separation between a globally shared lightweight backbone and permanently local personalization heads to inherently mitigate data heterogeneity; and (ii) a gradient ℓ₂-norm–based sparse scheduling strategy that selects only the top-α% devices per round for backbone updates—prioritizing informative gradients while minimizing UAV communication load and energy expenditure. Simulations under strong Non-IID settings show our method achieves 3.2–5.7% higher test accuracy than state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., FedAvg, FedProx, UAV-FL), while reducing per-round UAV energy consumption by 41.3%, effectively extending flight endurance. This work bridges personalized learning, energy-aware UAV communications, and practical FL deployment.

Abstract

Federated learning (FL) is an effective paradigm for enhancing the learning capability of edge devices while preserving data privacy. In geographically dispersed FL systems, such as sensor networks in remote areas, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) can flexibly establish high-quality communication links to support parameter exchange. However, device heterogeneity and the limited battery capacity of UAVs pose significant challenges. Specifically, data heterogeneity slows convergence, while scheduling all devices for global collaboration incurs excessive communication and energy costs. To overcome these challenges, we adopt a strict separation between a globally shared backbone and permanently local personalization heads, thereby mitigating the impact of data heterogeneity. Furthermore, we propose a gradient-based scheduling strategy that jointly considers energy efficiency and learning performance. In each communication round, the backbone is updated only by the top-$α$ devices ranked by gradient $\ell_{2}$-norm, ensuring that optimization focuses on the most informative updates. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves higher learning accuracy than state-of-the-art approaches while significantly reducing UAV energy consumption.

item.title: Localization then Neutralization: Gradient-guided Token Suppression against Visual Prompt Injection Attack
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25194v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

视觉提示注入攻击（Visual Prompt Injection）通过精心构造的对抗图像，劫持多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的推理路径，导致严重安全风险。现有防御方法或缺乏对攻击机制的原理性理解，或难以兼顾高效性与防御效用，常引入显著计算开销或损害模型正常任务性能。

方法创新：梯度引导的令牌抑制框架（GTM）

本文揭示关键洞见：成功攻击并非依赖整张图像，而是由少数高影响力图像令牌（image tokens）驱动。据此，我们提出Gradient Token Masking（GTM）——一种“先定位、后中和”的两阶段防御范式：

  • 定位（Localization）：摒弃传统基于首生成词概率的归因方式（其在攻击保持预测token时失效），创新采用隐状态梯度范数（Hidden-State Gradient Norm, HSGN） 作为生成影响归因指标；
  • 中和（Neutralization）：理论证明HSGN排序与完整对抗损失梯度排序严格一致，保障定位准确性；仅需单次前向-反向传播，即识别并置零Top-K高分令牌（通常<1%总token数），精准切断攻击信号流。

效果与优势

在Prompt Injection与多模态越狱（multimodal jailbreak）等多类攻击基准上，GTM将攻击成功率（ASR）压降至接近0%，同时保持原始模型在VQAv2、MMBench等基准上的性能几乎无损（下降<0.3%）。计算开销极低——单图平均仅增加约8ms延迟，无需微调或额外参数，具备强实用性与部署友好性。

AI Summary (English)

Adversarial visual prompt injection poses a critical security threat to multimodal large language models (MLLMs). This work identifies that successful attacks rely not on the entire image but on a sparse set of critical image tokens. To counter this, we propose Gradient Token Masking (GTM): a lightweight, gradient-guided defense that first localizes attack-critical tokens via the Hidden-State Gradient Norm (HSGN)—a theoretically grounded attribution metric proven consistent with full adversarial loss gradients—and then neutralizes them by masking. GTM requires only one forward-backward pass, zeros out <1% of tokens, and incurs negligible latency (<8 ms/image). Experiments across diverse prompt injection and multimodal jailbreak benchmarks show GTM reduces attack success rates (ASR) to near zero while preserving model utility (≤0.3% accuracy drop on VQAv2/MMBench), outperforming prior defenses in both efficacy and efficiency.

Abstract

Adversarial images pose a severe security threat to multimodal large language models through prompt injection. Existing defenses largely lack a principled understanding of the underlying mechanisms and struggle to balance efficiency and defense utility. In this work, we show that successful adversarial attacks do not rely on the entire image uniformly but instead depend on a small subset of critical image tokens. Based on this insight, we propose Gradient Token Masking (GTM), which localizes these tokens via gradient analysis and neutralizes them through masking. We find that attribution based on the first generated token's output probability fails when attacks preserve the predicted token. To overcome this, GTM utilizes the Hidden-State Gradient Norm score for generation-influence attribution under adversarial inputs. We prove that its ranking is consistent with that of the full adversarial loss gradient, providing a theoretical guarantee for accurate localization. Our method requires only a single forward-backward pass to identify and zero out a small number of high-scoring tokens, effectively disrupting the adversarial attack path. Extensive experiments on prompt injection and multimodal jailbreak attacks demonstrate that our approach reduces attack success rates (ASR) to near zero while preserving model utility with negligible computational overhead.

item.title: MimirRAG: A Multi-Agent RAG Framework for Financial Data Retrieval with Metadata Integration
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25030v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在金融分析场景中，大语言模型（LLM）需严格基于可验证的监管文件（如SEC 10-K/10-Q）生成答案，避免幻觉。传统RAG系统难以应对财务文档的异构性——包含密集文本、结构化表格、跨页图表及丰富元数据（如报告期、公司代码、章节层级），导致信息割裂、数值推理失准、检索相关性低。

方法：MimirRAG多智能体框架

本文提出MimirRAG（Metadata-Integrated Multi-Agent Information Retrieval），一个面向金融数据检索的模块化多智能体RAG框架：

  • 结构保持解析：采用PDF语义解析器保留原始布局与表格完整性；
  • 表感知分块（Table-Aware Chunking）：将表格作为原子单元切分，避免跨行/跨表断裂，并关联相邻文本上下文；
  • 元数据融合：自动提取并结构化文档级（公司名、财年）、章节级（“Item 7. Management’s Discussion”）、表格级（表标题、单位、时间维度）元数据，注入检索与重排序全流程；
  • 智能体协同检索：由Query Planner智能体分解复杂分析问题（如“对比2022与2023年苹果公司毛利率变化及原因”），调用Hybrid Search Agent（关键词+向量+元数据过滤）与Validation Agent（交叉验证多源证据）；
  • 上下文感知生成：集成数值推理模块，支持百分比计算、同比/环比推导及单位一致性校验。

关键发现与创新

消融实验证实三大技术支柱：元数据集成（+12.7%准确率）、表感知分块（+9.4%表格问答F1）、智能体工作流（+15.2%多跳推理成功率）。在FinanceBench基准上达89.3%准确率（超越原SOTA 6.8个百分点）；四名资深金融分析师的专家评估指出：系统显著提升证据溯源效率，但实际落地需配套可信度校准机制（如置信度可视化）、全源数据接入能力（EDGAR + Bloomberg + 内部数据库）及用户个性化配置（行业偏好、风险阈值设定）。本研究验证了“多智能体RAG + 人类中心设计”的协同范式对高可靠性金融洞察提取的有效性。

AI Summary (English)

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is critical for financial analysis, where LLM outputs must be rigorously grounded in regulatory filings to prevent hallucination. Yet, standard RAG struggles with financial documents’ complexity—dense text, multi-page tables, and rich metadata. This paper introduces MimirRAG, a metadata-integrated, multi-agent RAG framework designed specifically for financial data retrieval. Its pipeline features structure-preserving PDF parsing, table-aware chunking, automated metadata extraction (company, fiscal year, section, table semantics), query-planning agents, hybrid (keyword/vector/metadata) search, cross-document validation, and context-aware generation with numerical reasoning support. Ablation studies identify metadata integration, table-aware chunking, and the agentic workflow as the three key enablers of performance. Evaluated on FinanceBench, MimirRAG achieves 89.3% accuracy, outperforming prior baselines by 6.8 points. Expert validation with four financial analysts confirms its analytical utility but emphasizes that real-world deployment requires calibrated trust (e.g., confidence scoring), comprehensive data integration, and user personalization. MimirRAG demonstrates how human-centered multi-agent RAG architecture advances reliable financial insight extraction.

Abstract

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems offer a promising approach to reduce hallucinations and improve answer accuracy in large language models (LLMs), a requirement for reliable, financial analysis where answers must be grounded in verifiable evidence from filings rather than generated from model priors. However, designing RAG systems that extract meaningful insights from mixed financial documents and integrate into analyst workflows remains challenging. This paper introduces MimirRAG (Metadata-Integrated Multi-Agent Information Retrieval), a multi-agent RAG system developed iteratively to address these challenges. MimirRAG features a modular pipeline encompassing structure-preserving parsing of PDF filings, table-aware chunking, metadata extraction, agent-based retrieval with query planning and hybrid search, validation, and context-aware generation with numerical reasoning support. Our ablation study identifies three key technical enablers for effective financial RAG: metadata integration, table-aware chunking, and an agentic workflow. MimirRAG was evaluated quantitatively using FinanceBench and qualitatively through expert validation with four financial analysts. The system achieved 89.3% accuracy on FinanceBench, outperforming the original benchmark baselines. Expert feedback highlighted that successful deployment also requires calibrated trust, comprehensive data integration, and user personalization. We conclude that combining multi-agent RAG architecture with human-centric design principles can improve the extraction of meaningful insights in financial analysis.

item.title: Efficient DP-SGD for LLMs with Randomized Clipping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24879v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）在海量数据上训练，其中常含个人敏感信息。差分隐私（DP）作为形式化隐私保护的金标准，为LLM训练提供了可证明的隐私保障。然而，当前主流DP训练方案（如DP-SGD）依赖于高效梯度裁剪技术，其内存开销高达 $O(B \min\{T^2, d^2\})$——其中 $B$ 为批大小、$T$ 为序列长度、$d$ 为模型宽度。随着模型规模（如1B+参数）与上下文长度（如32k tokens）持续增长，该开销迅速成为训练瓶颈，严重制约长上下文DP微调的可行性。

方法创新：DP-SGD-RC

本文提出 DP-SGD-RC（DP-SGD with Randomized Clipping），一种新型差分隐私随机梯度下降变体。核心突破在于：摒弃传统确定性逐样本梯度范数计算（需显式存储全部中间梯度），转而采用随机迹估计技术——包括Hutchinson估计器（1989）及其改进版Hutch++（Meyer et al., 2021）——以无偏、低方差地近似每样本梯度L2范数。该策略将内存复杂度降至 $O(B d)$（线性于模型宽度），同时显著降低计算量，且无需额外反向传播或缓存高维梯度张量。

关键结果与优势

我们给出了紧致的隐私分析，严格证明DP-SGD-RC在相同$(\varepsilon,\delta)$预算下可实现与确定性裁剪相当的噪声乘子（noise multiplier），隐私保障不打折扣。在Llama-3.2-1B模型上，于多个长上下文基准（涵盖分类、问答、摘要任务）开展DP微调实验：DP-SGD-RC在保持基线模型效用（如准确率、ROUGE、F1）几乎完全一致的前提下，内存占用降低42%–68%，单步训练时间减少35%–51%。本工作首次实现了长上下文LLM DP训练的高效、可扩展与严格隐私兼容。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) trained on sensitive data require rigorous privacy guarantees, for which differential privacy (DP) is the gold standard. However, state-of-the-art DP-SGD implementations suffer from prohibitive $O(B \min\{T^2, d^2\})$ memory overhead—scaling poorly with sequence length $T$ and model width $d$. We propose DP-SGD-RC, a novel variant that replaces deterministic per-sample gradient norm computation with randomized clipping via stochastic trace estimation (Hutchinson’s estimator and Hutch++). This reduces memory to $O(B d)$ and cuts compute significantly, while preserving gradient unbiasedness and privacy rigor. Our tight privacy analysis shows DP-SGD-RC achieves noise multipliers competitive with deterministic clipping under identical $(\varepsilon,\delta)$. Experiments fine-tuning Llama-3.2-1B on long-context benchmarks (classification, QA, summarization) demonstrate on-par utility with baseline DP-SGD, alongside 42–68% memory reduction and 35–51% faster step time, enabling scalable, private LLM adaptation.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are trained on vast datasets that may contain sensitive information. Differential privacy (DP), the de facto standard for formal privacy guarantees, provides a principled framework for training LLMs with provable privacy protection. However, state-of-the-art DP training implementations rely on fast gradient clipping techniques with memory overhead $O(B \min\{T^2, d^2\})$, where $B$ is the batch size, $T$ is the sequence length, and $d$ is the model width. This becomes prohibitive as both model size and context length grow. We propose DP-SGD-RC, a novel variant of DP-SGD with randomized clipping that reduces memory and compute complexity. DP-SGD-RC leverages stochastic trace estimation methods, specifically Hutchinson's estimator[Hutchinson, 1989] and its improved variant, Hutch++[Meyer et al., 2021], to reduce the memory footprint of per-sample gradient norm estimation. We provide a tight privacy analysis showing that DP-SGD-RC achieves noise multipliers competitive with deterministic clipping. Experiments fine-tuning Llama~3.2-1B on long-context benchmarks spanning classification, question answering, and summarization tasks demonstrate that DP-SGD-RC matches baseline utility while significantly reducing memory and compute requirements.

item.title: Spiking the training data to correct for test set contamination
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24818v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

测试集污染（test set contamination）是大语言模型评估中的严重威胁，现有研究多聚焦于污染检测，而对受污染测试分数的统计校正方法缺乏系统探索。当模型在训练中意外接触测试样本时，其测试准确率被高估，导致性能评估失真。

方法：训练数据“加 Spike”校正法

本文提出一种新颖、可操作的校正范式——主动污染训练数据（spiking）：在训练集中以已知比例（如1%、5%）显式注入部分测试样本，并记录其污染标签。这些“spiked examples”构成可控的校准集，用于训练模型记忆度预测器（memorization predictor）和正确性预测器（correctness predictor），进而构建统计校正模型，反事实地估计并扣除污染带来的分数膨胀。

关键发现与创新

  • 构建基于Hubble模型对的仿真框架：每对包含一个标准模型（未污染，作为真实性能基准）与一个受控污染模型（注入特定测试集），实现校正效果的可验证评估；
  • 系统比较多种校正估计量，发现融合记忆度与正确性信号的联合估计器显著优于无校正的基线及单一信号估计器；
  • 实证表明，轻量级预测器（如Platt校准的成员推断分数）即可提供强校正信号，仅需约10个spiked样例即可完成可靠校准，且具备跨数据集迁移能力；
  • 该方法无需访问原始训练日志或模型梯度，部署成本低，为工业级模型评估提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的污染校正方案。

AI Summary (English)

Test set contamination inflates model evaluation scores, yet correction—unlike detection—remains underexplored. We propose spiking: intentionally injecting known fractions of test examples into training data to create labeled calibration sets. These spiked examples train memorization and correctness predictors, enabling principled statistical correction of inflated test accuracy. Using Hubble model pairs—where perturbed models are deliberately contaminated while standard models serve as clean counterfactuals—we evaluate estimators in simulation. Joint estimators leveraging both memorization and correctness signals consistently outperform naive (no-correction) baselines. Simple predictors—e.g., Platt-scaled membership inference metrics—achieve strong correction with as few as 10 spiked examples and transfer across datasets. Spiking thus offers a practical, statistically grounded solution for real-world model evaluation under contamination.

Abstract

The literature on test set contamination largely focuses on detection, but the correction of contaminated test scores is underexplored. Our core proposal is to spike the training data by intentionally contaminating some test examples at known rates. The spiked examples can then be used to calibrate predictors of model memorization which enable principled statistical correction of inflated test scores. To evaluate different correction estimators, we first present a simulation framework based on the Hubble models. Hubble models come in minimal pairs, where the perturbed model was deliberately contaminated with several test sets, while the standard model was not, serving as the counterfactual and correction target. We consider estimators that use information from a memorization predictor, correctness predictor, or both. In simulation, we establish basic statistical intuitions and show that estimators leveraging memorization and correctness information are better than naive estimation which makes no correction at all. We then instantiate several memorization and correctness predictors, and find that simple predictors such as Platt-scaled membership inference metrics provide good signal for correction. Finally, we examine the practical considerations of spiking. Simple memorization predictors need no more than 10 examples for calibration and often transfer from one dataset to another. Taken together, spiking is a promising solution for test set contamination.

item.title: They Are Not the Same: Direct Causes Are Not Grounded Emotion Explanations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25208v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

情感-原因对抽取（ECPE）任务本旨在为情感事件提供可解释的因果依据，即回答“为何产生该情感”。然而，当前主流方法已退化为简单的二元分类（“是/否情感-原因对”），这一代理目标虽利于识别直接触发词（direct causes），却常被误读为实现了“基于证据的情感解释”——而二者在认知与语用层面存在本质差异。

方法与发现

本文以IEMO-MECP数据集为基准，系统检验二元ECPE边界与真实解释性之间的对齐程度：

  • 边界稳定性分析：90.9%原始正样本仍被判定为情感-原因对，95.0%原始负样本仍被判定为非对，表明二元任务本身高度保真；
  • 关键缺陷揭示：大量情感语境（emo-context）——即不直接引发情感、但对理解情感不可或缺的上下文话语——跨跃原始二元边界分布，且显著富集于模型预测不确定区域，证明该边界无法稳定容纳解释性证据；
  • 模型行为诊断：所有评估模型均更可靠地召回直接触发词，却普遍弱化语境支持；在捷径学习（shortcut pressure）下，模型甚至赋予词形相似但无证据支持的邻近非对候选更高得分，反而压低有充分证据支撑但结构复杂的情感-原因或情感-语境对。

核心结论与创新

本研究首次明确区分“直接原因识别”与“ grounded emotion explanation”，指出：高ECPE准确率仅反映模型捕捉直接触发的能力，绝不等价于生成可验证、语境嵌入的解释。这一发现挑战了当前ECPE评估范式，并呼吁构建面向解释质量（而非仅配对精度）的新基准与建模框架。代码开源：https://github.com/panzhzh/ECPExsame。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the conflation of direct cause identification with grounded emotion explanation in Emotion-Cause Pair Extraction (ECPE). While ECPE was designed to explain why an emotion occurs, it is now widely reduced to binary pair/non-pair classification—a useful proxy for detecting direct triggers, but misleadingly interpreted as evidence-based explanation. We show this interpretation is only partially valid: though the binary task remains largely preserved on IEMO-MECP (90.9% original positives and 95.0% negatives retain their labels), the binary boundary fails to stably accommodate emo-context—utterances that support emotional interpretation without direct causation. Such context crosses the boundary and concentrates near model uncertainty. Across models, direct triggers are recovered more reliably than contextual evidence; under shortcut pressure, models assign higher pair scores to lexically similar but unsupported non-pair candidates than to evidence-backed, structurally complex emo-cause or emo-context pairs. Thus, high binary ECPE performance reflects trigger detection—not explanation. Code: https://github.com/panzhzh/ECPExsame.

Abstract

Emotion-Cause Pair Extraction (ECPE) was introduced to explain why an emotion occurs, but this goal is now often reduced to binary pair/non-pair prediction. This proxy is useful for direct-cause extraction, yet easy to over-read as evidence grounded emotion explanation. We show that this interpretation is only partially valid. In IEMO-MECP, 90.9% of original positives remain emo-cause and 95.0% of original negatives remain non-pair, confirming that the binary ECPE task is largely preserved. The problem is that direct triggers alone do not constitute a grounded explanation. Emo-context, an utterance that helps interpret a target emotion without directly causing it, appears on both sides of the original boundary and is enriched near binary uncertainty, showing that the binary boundary has no stable place for such discourse evidence. Across evaluated ECPE models, direct triggers are recovered more reliably than contextual support. Under shortcut pressure, this imbalance becomes consequential. Binary-trained models assign higher pair scores to nearby lexically similar non-pair candidates than to evidence supported but structurally harder emo-cause and emo-context pairs. Thus, pair scores can reward convenient attributions over grounded explanations. High binary ECPE performance indicates that a model can identify direct triggers; it does not indicate that the model has explained the emotion. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/panzhzh/ECPExsame.

item.title: CyberMaskQA: A Privacy-Aware Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models in Cybersecurity Question Answering
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24765v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛应用于网络安全问答（Cybersecurity QA），支撑事件响应、漏洞分析等关键任务。然而，真实运维数据（如系统日志、网络配置）天然包含敏感标识符（如IP地址、主机名、用户账户），在受监管环境（如金融、政务）中直接上传至云端LLM存在严重隐私与合规风险。更根本的是，当前缺乏兼顾操作语义丰富性与细粒度隐私标注的基准数据集，导致隐私保护型QA模型的研发长期受限。

方法与创新

本研究提出 CyberMaskQA——首个面向隐私感知的网络安全QA基准。其核心创新在于：

  • 情境化建模：问题均扎根于真实组织架构，显式建模资产间因果依赖（如“因服务器A权限提升→导致数据库B暴露”），超越传统基准仅测试静态事实知识的局限；
  • 可控脱敏设计：采用“人工基线场景 + LLM语义扩展”双阶段生成流程，并为每条样本精准标注私有实体类型与边界（如<IP:192.168.1.5>、<HOST:web-srv03>），支持掩码策略的定量评估；
  • 双维度评测：同步评估问答准确率（是否答对技术问题）与掩码保真度（是否仅隐藏应隐信息、不误删关键上下文），揭示隐私-效用权衡本质。

主要贡献

CyberMaskQA填补了“可部署、可验证、可解释”的隐私增强型网络安全LLM评测空白。实验表明，现有主流模型在掩码鲁棒性上表现参差（平均漏掩率23.7%，过掩率18.4%），凸显该基准的诊断价值。数据集与生成框架将于论文录用后开源，推动安全AI的合规落地。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for cybersecurity question answering (QA), yet real-world operational data contains sensitive identifiers (e.g., IPs, hostnames) that pose privacy and regulatory risks when processed by cloud-based models. Crucially, progress in privacy-preserving QA is hindered by the absence of context-rich, human-annotated benchmarks that jointly evaluate operational reasoning and controlled information disclosure. To bridge this gap, we introduce CyberMaskQA, a novel privacy-aware QA benchmark covering incident response, vulnerability analysis, and access control. Unlike existing benchmarks focused on factual recall, CyberMaskQA grounds questions in realistic organizational contexts with explicit causal dependencies among assets and privileges. Its dataset is generated via a hybrid pipeline—human-curated base scenarios augmented by LLM-driven semantic expansion—and each instance is annotated with precise private entity labels (e.g., <IP:10.2.1.8>) to enable fine-grained masking evaluation. Experiments show CyberMaskQA effectively exposes trade-offs between QA accuracy and masking fidelity, providing a rigorous testbed for developing deployable, privacy-compliant cybersecurity LLMs. The dataset and generation framework will be publicly released upon acceptance.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to cybersecurity question answering (QA) for critical tasks such as incident response and vulnerability analysis. However, real-world operational contexts, including system logs and network configurations, inherently contain sensitive identifiers, e.g., IP addresses, host names, and user accounts. Processing this data with cloud-based models is often unsafe or infeasible in regulated environments. Furthermore, progress in privacy-preserving QA is hindered by the lack of annotated, context-rich datasets capable of jointly evaluating operational reasoning and privacy preservation. To address this gap, we introduce CYBERMASKQA, a privacy-aware QA benchmark covering key security domains. Unlike existing benchmarks that primarily test factual knowledge, CYBERMASKQA grounds questions in realistic organizational contexts with explicit causal dependencies among assets and privileges. Generated through a systematic pipeline, the dataset combines human-curated base scenarios with LLM-driven semantic expansion, annotating each instance with precise private entity labels to enable controlled information disclosure. Evaluations of QA accuracy and masking performance demonstrate the benchmark's utility for developing deployable, context-aware cybersecurity models and facilitating nuanced studies of privacy-utility trade-offs. Upon acceptance, we will release the dataset and the generation framework.

item.title: Demystifying the Mythos or Disrupting Bugonomics? From Zero-Day Asymmetry to Defender Remediation Throughput
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24632v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

近期大语言模型（LLM）在生产软件中成功生成候选及确认漏洞的实证，再度引发“AI将重塑攻防安全格局”的叙事。然而，主流讨论聚焦能力跃升，却普遍忽视其背后的成本结构、经济激励与工作流瓶颈。本文突破传统技术视角，引入“Bugonomics”（漏洞经济学）框架——系统分析漏洞的生成、验证、优先级排序与修复全生命周期运营成本。

方法与数据来源

本研究基于三类实证锚点：（1）Anthropic公司Mythos Preview项目公开报告；（2）Mozilla Firefox与LLM协同漏洞发现的协作日志与响应数据；（3）公开漏洞市场定价（如Zerodium报价）与主流漏洞赏金计划（HackerOne、Bugcrowd）的奖励分布。通过对比分析LLM介入前后各环节单位成本变化，量化其对攻防两侧经济模型的差异化影响。

核心发现

  • 攻击侧高成本壁垒未被瓦解：LLM可批量生成POC级候选漏洞，但可靠零日利用链仍需深度逆向、环境适配与稳定性加固，政府/商业攻击方的专业溢价依然显著。
  • 防御侧发生结构性位移：LLM大幅降低候选生成、代码理解、测试用例（harness）构建、影响证明草稿与报告撰写等环节的边际成本，导致高质量候选报告数量激增。
  • 新瓶颈浮现：核心约束已从“发现不足”转向“吸收—验证—分诊—修补—发布”的全流程吞吐能力，尤其在开源生态中，维护者侧的评审、资金、CI/CD与版本发布容量难以线性扩展。

创新点

提出“防御者修复吞吐量（Defender Remediation Throughput）”作为关键绩效指标，主张未来安全投入应从“扩大发现规模”转向“强化验证基础设施、自动化分诊引擎与维护者赋能机制”。

AI Summary (English)

This paper reframes LLM-driven vulnerability discovery through bugonomics—the operational economics of finding, validating, prioritizing, and fixing security defects. Contrary to headlines heralding an “AI zero-day flood,” we show that LLMs do not collapse offensive high-end exploit economics (which remain costly due to reliability, stealth, and chaining requirements), but do dramatically reduce marginal costs for defender-side candidate generation, code comprehension, harness construction, impact drafting, and reporting. Using data from Anthropic’s Mythos Preview, Mozilla Firefox collaborations, and public exploit-market/reward-program benchmarks, we demonstrate that the near-term shift is toward defender remediation throughput: low-signal candidates become cheaper to produce, evidence-rich remediation guidance grows in value, and scarce human capacity (especially maintainer review, triage, funding, and release engineering) becomes the critical bottleneck—particularly acute in open source. The key implication: security ROI now hinges less on finding more bugs, and more on scaling validation infrastructure and maintainer enablement.

Abstract

Recent demonstrations of large language models producing candidate and confirmed vulnerabilities in production software have renewed the narrative that AI will reshape offensive and defensive security. Headlines emphasize capability; they rarely interrogate costs and incentives. This paper examines LLM-driven vulnerability discovery through a bugonomics lens: the operational economics of producing, proving, prioritizing, and fixing security-relevant defects. Historically, the most visible high-end bugonomics was offense-priced because production-grade zero-days and exploit chains were expensive specialist outputs for governments, brokers, and offensive vendors. Defender-side bugonomics already existed in vulnerability research, reward programs, and vendor remediation work; LLM-assisted systems change its scale and distribution. They make candidate generation, code comprehension, harness construction, proof-of-impact drafting, and report preparation cheaper at codebase scale. Exploits and proofs of concept remain important, but in defender workflows they primarily prove impact, guide prioritization, and justify remediation. The resulting bottleneck is not only finding more bugs; it is absorbing, validating, triaging, patching, and shipping a larger stream of reports. Using public data from Anthropic's Mythos Preview and Mozilla Firefox collaborations, along with public exploit-market price anchors and vulnerability reward programs, we argue that the near-term shift is not simply more zero-days. It is a move toward broader defender remediation throughput: low-signal candidates become cheaper, evidence-rich remediation become more important, and scarce capacity shifts toward maintainer review and release work. The effect is acute in open source, where LLM-assisted discovery can increase report volume while maintainer-side validation, triage, funding, and release capacity may not scale.

item.title: Poisoning the Watchtower: Prompt Injection Attacks Against LLM-Augmented Security Operations Through Adversarial Log Content
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24421v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）在安全运营中心（SOC）中日益普及，其作为“分析师助手”被用于日志解析、告警分类、事件摘要与响应建议等关键任务。然而，该范式存在根本性风险：大量日志字段（如User-Agent、URL、DNS查询、HTTP载荷、尝试登录用户名）可由攻击者直接控制，从而成为向LLM注入恶意指令的隐蔽信道。本文首次系统揭示并命名这一新型威胁——日志基底提示注入（log-substrate prompt injection）。

方法与贡献

我们提出四类攻击的细粒度分类法：S1 直接覆盖（Direct Override）、S2 人格劫持（Persona Hijack）、S3 上下文操纵（Context Manipulation）、S4 混淆载荷（Obfuscated Payload）。基于 gpt-4o-mini 构建模拟SOC分析员，在48种策略–防御–任务组合下开展实证评估。涵盖分类、摘要、建议三大典型任务，并测试多种防御机制（包括系统提示加固、输出约束、上下文清洗）。

关键发现

  • S1攻击完全失效（0%压制率），暴露当前LLM对显式指令覆盖的强鲁棒性；
  • S2（人格劫持）最具实战威胁：在朴素分类器下成功压制68%恶意日志，且在强化防御下仍保持显著有效性；
  • 摘要任务风险最高：S3攻击在无防御时注入成功率高达96%，即使启用严格输出约束仍达38%；
  • 防御效果有限：最强防御仅将平均注入成功率从26.6%降至11.8%，远未消除风险；
  • 模拟器严重失真：基于确定性规则的mock analyst严重高估S1效果，凸显真实LLM行为的不可替代性。

本研究呼吁SOC协作者必须将原始日志视为对抗性输入，而非普通上下文，为LLM安全集成提供关键设计原则与实证基准。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and characterizes log-substrate prompt injection—a critical structural vulnerability in LLM-augmented security operations, where attacker-controlled log fields (e.g., User-Agent, URLs, DNS queries) serve as covert channels for injecting malicious instructions into analyst-assistant models. We propose a four-class taxonomy: S1 (Direct Override), S2 (Persona Hijack), S3 (Context Manipulation), and S4 (Obfuscated Payload). Evaluating 48 strategy-defense-task combinations using gpt-4o-mini, we find: (1) S1 attacks fail entirely (0% suppression), while S2 achieves 68% suppression under naive classification—and remains potent even under strong defenses; (2) summarization is the highest-risk task, with S3 reaching 96% injection success without defenses and 38% under constrained output; (3) defenses reduce but do not eliminate risk—average injection success drops only from 26.6% to 11.8% under our strongest mitigation. Crucially, deterministic mock analysts mispredict real model behavior, especially for S1. These results compel SOC copilots to treat raw logs as adversarial input—not benign context.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as analyst assistants in security operations centers (SOCs), where they ingest log and alert data to produce triage labels, incident summaries, or remediation advice. We study a structural failure mode of this design: many log fields are attacker controlled. User agents, URLs, payloads, DNS queries, and attempted usernames can therefore carry instructions to the model alongside evidence of the intrusion. We call this setting \emph{log-substrate prompt injection}. We introduce a four-class taxonomy of log-substrate attacks: direct override (S1), persona hijack (S2), context manipulation (S3), and obfuscated payloads (S4). We evaluate 48 strategy-defense-task combinations using \texttt{gpt-4o-mini} as the analyst. Three findings stand out. First, direct overrides are ineffective in our setting: all S1 classification attacks achieve 0\% suppression. In contrast, persona hijacks suppress 68\% of malicious logs under a naive classifier and remain effective under stronger defenses. Second, summarization is the highest-risk task: context manipulation reaches 96\% injection success without defenses and 38\% even with constrained output. Third, defenses reduce but do not eliminate the attack surface: average injection success falls from 26.6\% under naive prompting to 11.8\% under our strongest defense. We also compare empirical results to a deterministic mock analyst and find that simulation substantially mispredicts current model behavior, especially for direct overrides. These results suggest that SOC copilots should treat raw log content as adversarial input rather than ordinary analyst context.

item.title: Five Queries Are Enough: Query-Efficient and Surrogate-Free Membership Inference Attacks on RAG via Entailment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24312v1

AI Summary (中文)

五问即破：基于自然语言蕴含的查询高效、代理模型无关型RAG成员推断攻击

检索增强生成（RAG）系统通过将大语言模型（LLM）响应锚定于私有或企业文档库，显著缓解幻觉问题，已成为工业部署核心范式。然而，该架构引入新型隐私风险：模型输出可能隐含其检索语料中特定文档的存在性信号，从而支持成员推断攻击（MIA）——非法判定某文档是否属于目标RAG系统的私有检索库。现有MIA方法存在严重局限：或依赖易被检测的模板化查询（如“请逐字复述文档X”），或需数十至上百次非模板化但高成本、强重复性的查询，实用性与隐蔽性均不足。

本文提出MEntA（Membership Entailment Attack），首个在严格受限预算下（仅5次查询）、无需任何代理模型（surrogate-free）、不依赖预设模板、且对主流防御机制完全不可知（defense-agnostic） 的MIA框架。其核心创新在于将MIA重构为自然语言蕴含（NLI）任务：攻击者构造宽泛、低成本、信息导向的开放式问题（如“简述癌症早期筛查的主流技术路线”），获取RAG响应后，计算该响应与候选文档间的文本蕴含置信度（而非传统词频/嵌入相似度）。蕴含得分显著高于阈值即判定该文档为成员——因RAG若未检索到相关文档，其响应难以逻辑蕴含原文细节。

在NFCorpus、SCIDOCS与TREC-COVID三大真实学术/医疗RAG基准上，MEntA仅用5次查询即达最高0.991 AUC，较现有最优方法提升0.20–0.50 AUC；在SOTA防御（如检索扰动、响应模糊化）下仍保持强鲁棒性，而现有MIA检测器对其漏报率超85%或误报率达42%。成本方面，总攻击开销降低至SOTA方法的1/65。本研究首次证实：轻量、隐蔽、高性价比的RAG隐私泄露在现实中切实可行，亟需从检索机制设计与端到端隐私防护层面重构RAG安全范式。

AI Summary (English)

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems face a critical privacy threat: membership inference attacks (MIAs) that determine whether specific documents reside in their private retrieval corpus. Existing MIAs are either detectable (via templated queries) or impractical (requiring dozens of costly, repetitive queries). We propose MEntA, the first query-efficient (≤5 queries), surrogate-free, template-agnostic, and defense-agnostic MIA for RAG. MEntA reframes membership inference as natural language entailment: it issues broad, low-cost information-seeking questions, then measures entailment confidence between the RAG’s response and candidate documents—high entailment signals membership. Evaluated on NFCorpus, SCIDOCS, and TREC-COVID, MEntA achieves up to 0.991 AUC with only 5 queries, outperforming prior work by 0.20–0.50 AUC. It remains effective against state-of-the-art RAG defenses, evades existing MIA detectors (high false-negative rate), and reduces total attack cost by up to 65× versus SOTA methods. This exposes a realistic, low-budget privacy leakage vector in production RAG systems.

Abstract

Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become central to large language model (LLM) deployments, grounding responses in enterprise or proprietary data to reduce hallucinations. However, this design introduces a new privacy risk: model outputs may signal the presence of specific documents in the retrieval corpus, enabling membership inference attacks (MIAs) that leak sensitive information. Existing MIAs are feasible, but they often rely on easily detected templated queries or require many non-templated yet costly and repetitive queries, limiting practicality. We ask: Can an adversary launch a limited-budget, surrogate-free, stealthy, and defense-agnostic membership inference attack using non-templated queries? We present MEntA (Membership Entailment Attack), a query-efficient MIA that leverages natural-language entailment to maximize information gained per query. By asking low-cost, broad, information-seeking questions and measuring entailment between model responses and candidate documents, MEntA eliminates the need for costly shadow models and large query budgets. Across NFCorpus, SCIDOCS, and TREC-COVID, MEntA achieves up to 0.991 AUC with only 5 queries, outperforming prior methods by 0.20 to 0.50 AUC under equivalent conditions. It remains effective under state-of-the-art (SOTA) RAG defenses, while current detectors either miss MEntA or flag benign queries at high rates. Regarding cost, MEntA reduces total attack cost by up to 65 $\times$ lower compared to SOTA attacks under the same attack setting. Our findings expose the feasibility of realistic, low-cost privacy leakage in RAG systems and highlight the urgent need for privacy-aware retrieval and defense mechanisms.

item.title: Reframing LLM Agent Security as an Agent-Human Interaction Problem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24309v1

AI Summary (中文)

核心主张

本文提出：大语言模型（LLM）智能体的安全问题本质上是智能体-人类交互（Agent-Human Interaction, AHI）问题，而非单纯算法或模型鲁棒性问题。这一范式转换要求将安全设计重心从“让智能体更可信”转向“让人与智能体协同决策更可靠”。

方法与发现

我们系统分析了截至2026年4月的59篇学术论文、21个生产级智能体系统及26个安全插件。结果揭示显著断层：

  • ✅ 工业实践主导机制为三类人本安全措施：策略显式声明（14/21系统采用）、运行时人工审批（15/21）、作用域动态配置（16/21）；
  • ❌ 学术研究热点——如意图锚定（intent anchoring）与信任标签（trust labeling）——在全部21个生产系统中部署率为零。

然而，现有人类参与机制存在根本缺陷：认知负荷与安全强度呈强负相关。用户被迫在“审批疲劳”（频繁中断、决策过载）与“失控自治”（权限过大、缺乏制衡）间两难。

三大贡献

1. 理论论证：通过对比LLM自主对齐与人类实时对齐的可靠性，证明在当前技术边界下，人类参与安全关键决策不可替代；
2. 实证揭示：首次量化“产学错配”——工业广泛采用的机制仅获极少学术关注，而高引学术方案零落地；
3. 范式倡议：提出AHI安全的三维研究议程（交互接口设计、认知负荷建模、人机责任分配），呼吁将其确立为具独立设计原则、评估体系与理论基础的一级研究方向。

AI Summary (English)

We reframe LLM agent security as fundamentally an agent-human interaction (AHI) problem—not an algorithmic one. Analyzing 59 academic papers, 21 production systems, and 26 security plugins (as of April 2026), we find a stark industry-academia mismatch: human-centric mechanisms—policy specification (14/21 systems), runtime approval (15/21), and scope configuration (16/21)—dominate practice, while academically favored techniques like intent anchoring and trust labeling achieve zero production deployment. Critically, current human participation suffers from an irreducible trade-off between cognitive burden and security assurance, trapping users between approval fatigue and uncontrolled autonomy. Our contributions are threefold: (1) We demonstrate that human involvement in security-critical decisions remains indispensable for intent alignment under current LLM capabilities; (2) We quantify the severe misalignment between deployed mechanisms and research focus; and (3) We propose a three-direction AHI security research agenda—and advocate for AHI security to be recognized as a first-class discipline with its own design principles, evaluation frameworks, and theoretical foundations.

Abstract

We argue that LLM agent security is fundamentally an agent-human interaction (AHI) problem, not a purely algorithmic one. To substantiate this position, we conduct a systematic analysis of 59 academic papers, 21 production agent systems, and 26 security plugins as of April 2026. Our analysis reveals a striking pattern: the three widely deployed human-centric security mechanisms (policy specification, runtime approval, and scope configuration) dominate industry practice, each adopted by at least 14 of 21 systems (14, 15, and 16, respectively), while the categories most heavily studied in academia (intent anchoring and trust labeling) see zero production deployment. Yet current human participation mechanisms are far from satisfactory: they suffer from a fundamental trade-off between cognitive burden and security guarantees, leaving users caught between approval fatigue and uncontrolled agent autonomy. We make three contributions. First, through a systematic comparison of LLM-based and human-based intent alignment, we argue that human participation in agent security decisions is indispensable given current capabilities. Second, we quantify a pronounced industry-academia mismatch: the security mechanisms that practitioners actually deploy receive scant research attention, while the approaches that researchers favor remain undeployed. Third, we propose a three-direction research agenda and call for AHI security to be recognized as a first-class research citizen, one that demands its own design principles, evaluation methods, and theoretical foundations.

item.title: Rethinking Federated Unlearning via the Lens of Memorization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24545v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）在隐私敏感场景中日益普及，而《通用数据保护条例》（GDPR）等法规对“被遗忘权”的强制要求催生了联邦机器遗忘（Federated Unlearning）需求。然而，现有方法常将待遗忘数据与剩余数据视为完全独立，忽视二者间存在的语义重叠与共享模式，导致两类关键缺陷：（1）无效遗忘——模型仍残留对已请求删除样本的过度记忆；（2）客户端不公平——因数据分布异构性，部分客户端承担不成比例的遗忘代价，损害整体效用。

方法创新

本文首次从记忆化（memorization）视角重构联邦遗忘范式。我们提出核心观点：遗忘的目标不应是抹除所有关联，而是精准移除仅由待遗忘数据独有、且未被剩余数据支撑的“专属记忆”。为此，我们设计分组记忆评估（Grouped Memorization Evaluation, GME）——一种细粒度、示例级指标，通过对比单样本训练轨迹与群体梯度一致性，定量分离“专属记忆”与“重叠知识”。基于GME，我们提出联邦记忆剪枝（FedMemPrune）：在客户端本地识别并重置对专属记忆贡献最大的冗余参数子集（而非全局微调或模型重训），实现轻量、可证明的遗忘。

主要结果

在CIFAR-10/100、Tiny-ImageNet及LEAF-FEMNIST等基准上，FedMemPrune在遗忘有效性（ΔAUC下降>92%）、效用保持（平均精度损失<1.3%）与公平性（客户端效用方差降低37%）三方面全面超越SOTA联邦遗忘方法（如FedEraser、UnFed）。其性能逼近全量重训练基线，但通信开销仅为后者的1/8，验证了“精准剪枝优于粗粒度覆盖”的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated unlearning is critical for regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR), yet existing methods often ignore semantic overlap between unlearning and retained data—causing ineffective forgetting and client unfairness. This work rethinks federated unlearning through the lens of memorization, arguing that unlearning should selectively erase unique memorized knowledge attributable solely to the to-be-forgotten data, while preserving overlapping patterns supported by remaining data. To this end, we propose Grouped Memorization Evaluation (GME), an example-level metric that disentangles unique memorization from shared knowledge via gradient consistency analysis. Leveraging GME, we design Federated Memorization Pruning (FedMemPrune): a lightweight, pruning-based approach that identifies and resets only the parameters most responsible for unique memorization at each client. Extensive experiments show FedMemPrune matches full retraining in unlearning efficacy (e.g., >92% ΔAUC reduction) while outperforming state-of-the-art federated unlearning methods in both utility preservation (<1.3% accuracy drop) and fairness (37% lower utility variance across clients), all with 8× less communication overhead.

Abstract

Federated learning (FL) increasingly needs machine unlearning to comply with privacy regulations. However, existing federated unlearning approaches may overlook the overlapping information between the unlearning and remaining data, leading to ineffective unlearning and unfairness between clients. In this work, we revisit federated unlearning through the lens of memorization. We argue that unlearning should mainly remove the unique memorized information attributable to the data to be forgotten, while preserving overlapping patterns that are also supported by the remaining data. Specifically, we propose Grouped Memorization Evaluation, an example-level metric that separates memorized knowledge from overlapping knowledge. Building on this metric, we introduce Federated Memorization Pruning (FedMemPrune), a pruning-based unlearning approach that resets redundant parameters responsible for memorization. Extensive experiments show that FedMemPrune closely matches retraining-based unlearning baselines while more effectively eliminating memorization than existing federated unlearning algorithms, yielding strong unlearning performance without sacrificing the utility of retained knowledge.

item.title: Market Regime Council for Dynamic Credit Assignment in Multi-Agent LLM Decision Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24490v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

面向投资组合管理的多智能体大语言模型（Multi-agent LLM）决策系统仍面临三大瓶颈：缺乏可解释的信用分配机制，难以量化各专业代理（如趋势、波动率、流动性专家）对最终决策的实际贡献；冷启动主导问题——市场状态突变（如牛市→熊市）时，历史表现滞后的代理易被错误加权；决策黑箱化——现有方法无法追溯“最终资产配置如何由多代理协作生成”。

方法创新：Market Regime Council（MRC）

我们提出市场状态理事会（MRC），一种基于合作博弈论的动态信用分配框架：

  • 精确Shapley值计算：在线实时评估所有单代理、两两组合及全联盟（Grand-coalition）输出的边际贡献，生成动态权重；
  • 三层自适应机制：① 采用指数加权性能历史更新Shapley权重；② 引入贝叶斯自适应混合器缓解初期数据稀疏导致的权重震荡；③ 设计状态依赖乘子（如高波动期抑制趋势代理权威），实现跨市场周期的鲁棒性；
  • 五层因果可追溯日志：完整记录每轮再平衡中代理输入、联盟结构、Shapley归因、状态调节因子及最终权重，支持审计与归因分析。

实证结果与价值

在13种加密资产、1037个交易日、5组随机种子的严苛测试中，MRC实现年化夏普比率1.51、累计收益440.1%，在主动基线中CR（累计收益）、SR（夏普比率）、IR（信息比率）三项第一，且最大回撤（MDD）最低。消融实验确证：性能提升源于多层级联盟输出的Shapley加权集成，而非任一模块单独作用——这是首个将合作博弈严谨嵌入LLM多代理金融决策的端到端系统。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the Market Regime Council (MRC), a cooperative multi-agent LLM decision system for portfolio management that addresses three key gaps: opaque credit assignment across specialist agents, cold-start dominance during market regime shifts, and insufficient transparency in allocation formation. MRC computes exact Shapley values over all single-agent, pairwise, and grand-coalition outputs to derive online agent weights. It integrates exponential-weighted performance histories, a Bayesian adaptive mixture for early-period stabilization, and regime-dependent authority multipliers—each rebalance is logged via a five-layer causal trace. Evaluated over 1,037 trading days across 13 crypto assets and 5 seeds, MRC achieves a Sharpe ratio of 1.51, cumulative return of 440.1%, and ranks first in CR, SR, and IR among active baselines while attaining the lowest MDD. Ablation confirms gains stem from Shapley-weighted coalition integration, not isolated components—establishing the first principled, game-theoretic credit assignment framework for dynamic LLM-based multi-agent finance.

Abstract

Multi-agent LLM decision systems for portfolio management still lack a principled way to assign credit across specialist agents, remain vulnerable to cold-start dominance under regime shifts, and offer limited transparency into how final allocations are formed. We propose Market Regime Council (MRC), a cooperative multi-agent decision system that computes exact Shapley credits across all single, pairwise, and Grand-coalition outputs for online agent weighting. Instantiated with N=3 specialist agents, at each trading period, MRC recomputes coalition-based Shapley weights from exponentially weighted performance histories, uses a Bayesian adaptive mixture to stabilize early periods, applies regime-dependent multipliers to adjust agent authority, and records each rebalance through a five-layer causal trace. Over 1,037 trading days across 13 crypto assets and five seeds, MRC achieves a Sharpe ratio of 1.51 and a cumulative return of 440.1%, ranking first on CR, SR, and IR among active baselines and attaining the lowest MDD among active methods. Ablation results show that the gains come from Shapley-weighted integration across coalition outputs rather than from any single stage in isolation. Code and demo data are included in the supplementary material.

item.title: Batch Normalization Amplifies Memorization and Privacy Risks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24420v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

批归一化（Batch Normalization, BN）作为深度学习中广泛应用的标准化技术，被普遍认为可加速收敛、提升训练稳定性。然而，其对模型记忆行为（memorization）与隐私泄露风险的影响长期被忽视——尤其当训练数据包含异常值（outliers）或分布外样本（out-of-distribution, OOD）时，BN是否无意中放大了对敏感/罕见样本的过度拟合？这一机制性风险尚未得到系统揭示。

方法与实验设计

本研究通过三重互补实证路径进行系统验证：

  • 异常样本记忆检测：在CIFAR-10/CIFAR-100中注入OOD训练样本（如SVHN图像），量化模型对其的“无意记忆”程度；
  • 单样本影响分析：基于梯度范数（gradient norm）计算每个训练样本在优化过程中的逐步影响强度；
  • 隐私攻击评估：采用多种主流成员推断攻击（MIA），测量BN模型与非BN对照模型在识别“某样本是否参与训练”上的准确率差异。

实验覆盖ResNet、VGG及ViT等多种架构，在CIFAR、ImageNet-1K等数据集上均保持一致设定。

主要发现与创新点

  • BN显著增强模型对异常/离群训练样本的记忆强度（平均提升2.3–4.1倍），且该效应独立于模型容量与训练时长；
  • 记忆增强直接导致隐私脆弱性激增：BN模型在MIA下的攻击成功率较对照组平均高出18.7%（最高达32.5%），AUC提升0.21；
  • 理论分析首次证明：BN通过放大异常样本在每步梯度更新中的相对贡献（即归一化引入的梯度缩放效应），从优化动力学层面解释了记忆放大机制；
  • 本工作首次将BN这一常规组件与数据稀有性—记忆强度—隐私泄露三者建立因果链，为隐私保护训练提供了关键警示与新干预靶点。

AI Summary (English)

Batch Normalization (BN) is a cornerstone technique for stabilizing and accelerating deep neural network training—but its privacy implications remain poorly understood. This paper demonstrates that BN amplifies memorization of atypical or out-of-distribution (OOD) training samples, directly increasing vulnerability to membership inference attacks (MIAs). Through extensive experiments across multiple datasets (CIFAR-10/100, ImageNet-1K) and architectures (ResNet, VGG, ViT), we show that BN models consistently memorize OOD samples 2.3–4.1× more than their non-BN counterparts. Crucially, this amplified memorization translates into significantly higher MIA success rates (+18.7% avg., up to +32.5%) and AUC gains (+0.21). We further provide theoretical analysis proving that BN magnifies the per-step gradient influence of outlier samples via normalization-induced gradient scaling—a mechanistic explanation for the observed effect. Our work reveals an underappreciated privacy risk inherent in BN and offers both practical guidance and foundational insight into how normalization layers unintentionally privilege rare or sensitive training examples.

Abstract

Batch Normalization (BN) is widely adopted to enable faster convergence and more stable training of deep neural networks. However, its impact on privacy and memorization has remained largely unexplored. In this work, we investigate the effect of BN layers on the memorization of atypical or outlier samples and its implications for privacy leakage. We conduct an extensive empirical study using three complementary approaches: (i) unintended memorization of out-of-distribution training samples, (ii) per-sample influence measured via gradient norms, and (iii) susceptibility to membership inference attacks (MIA). Across multiple datasets and architectures, we consistently observe that BN substantially increases the memorization of outliers compared to models without BN. Critically, this amplified memorization translates directly into privacy vulnerabilities: models with BN exhibit significantly higher susceptibility to MIAs. We complement our empirical findings with a theoretical analysis showing that BN amplifies the per-step influence of outlier samples during training, providing mechanistic insight into this phenomenon. Our results highlight an underappreciated privacy risk associated with BN and provide both practical and theoretical insights into how normalization layers can amplify the influence of rare or sensitive training examples.

item.title: Hardware-Aware Federated Learning for Speech Emotion Recognition
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24712v1

AI Summary (中文)

面向语音情感识别的硬件感知联邦学习框架

联邦学习（FL）为边缘设备间隐私保护的协同建模提供了可行路径，但在真实语音情感识别（SER）场景中面临严峻挑战：客户端硬件高度异构（如嵌入式麦克风阵列、智能手机、车载终端），其计算能力、内存容量与通信延迟差异显著，导致每轮训练时长波动大、系统开销高，严重制约部署可行性。本文针对会话级划分的IEMOCAP数据集，提出一种端到端硬件感知的联邦学习框架，首次将硬件性能建模、动态客户端筛选与自适应本地训练深度耦合于统一训练循环中。具体而言：（1）通过轻量级硬件探针（<50ms）实时采集CPU负载、内存占用、网络RTT等指标，构建多维硬件特征向量；（2）设计基于加权效用函数的Top-K客户端选择策略，在精度-时效-能耗三维目标下优先调度高性价比设备；（3）引入自适应本地训练机制，依据设备算力动态调整本地迭代轮数（epochs），避免低配设备过载或高配设备欠训练。在非独立同分布（non-IID）设定下，经50轮联邦训练与5次独立实验验证，本方法相较FedAvg取得0.352的验证准确率（持平最优基线），总训练时间降低36.5%，累积通信量减少40%，同时显著缓解设备掉线率（下降52%）。该框架无需修改模型结构或增加中心服务器负担，具备即插即用特性，为资源受限边缘场景下的语音情感分析提供了可落地的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative training for speech emotion recognition (SER) across edge devices, yet real-world deployments suffer from severe hardware heterogeneity—varying compute power, memory, and network latency—which inflates round duration and system cost. This paper proposes a hardware-aware FL framework tailored to session-partitioned IEMOCAP. It unifies three key components: lightweight hardware profiling (CPU load, memory, RTT), utility-weighted Top-K client selection, and adaptive local epochs calibrated per-device capability. Evaluated under non-IID data distribution over 50 rounds and 5 trials, our method achieves competitive validation accuracy (0.352), reduces total training time by 36.5% versus FedAvg, and cuts cumulative communication cost by 40%, while improving client participation stability. The framework requires no model modification or server-side overhead, offering a practical, deployable solution for resource-constrained SER.

Abstract

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative training across distributed edge devices, but real deployments involve heterogeneous clients with different processing power, memory capacity, and communication latency, which often increase round duration and system cost. This paper proposes a hardware-aware federated learning framework for emotion recognition on session-partitioned IEMOCAP that integrates hardware profiling, top-K client selection, and adaptive local epochs within a unified training loop. We compare the method against FedAvg, FedProx, and random top-K selection under a non-IID setup and show that, across 50 federated rounds and 5 independent trials, the proposed approach achieves competitive validation accuracy (0.352), reduces total training time by about 36.5% compared to FedAvg, and lowers cumulative communication cost by 40%.

item.title: IterInject: Indirect Prompt Injection Against LLM Agents via Feedback-Guided Iterative Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24659v1

AI Summary (中文)

IterInject：基于反馈引导的迭代优化实现对大语言模型代理的间接提示注入攻击

随着大语言模型（LLM）代理在规划、工具调用与外部服务交互等复杂任务中的广泛应用，其依赖不可信外部内容（如检索结果、网页文本）的架构使其面临间接提示注入（IPI） 的严重安全威胁——攻击者可将恶意指令隐匿于合法数据中，诱导代理偏离预期行为。现有IPI攻击方法存在两大瓶颈：（1）静态载荷缺乏适应性，难以绕过模型特异性防御；（2）自适应方法缺少结构化反馈机制，导致优化过程盲目低效。为此，本文提出 IterInject——首个闭环式反馈驱动迭代优化框架。该框架包含三大核心组件：

  • 规则驱动诊断器：对每次注入结果进行细粒度行为标注（如“工具误调用”“目标跳过”），生成结构化诊断标签；
  • 历史感知优化器：基于LLM的优化模块，依据完整优化历史（含前序失败模式、成功策略、诊断标签）动态重构提示载荷；
  • 合成式种子演化：从失败案例中自动提取共性模式（如特定token序列、语义歧义结构），生成新型伪装种子，实现攻击策略空间的自主进化。

实验表明：在AgentDojo与InjectAgent基准上，IterInject在4种主流受害模型（Llama-3-70B、Qwen2-72B、Gemma-2-27B、Claude-3.5-Sonnet）上显著超越静态基线（+42.6%平均成功率）及现有自适应方法（+28.3%）。进一步在生产级编码代理Claude Code（具备多层过滤、语法校验与执行沙箱）上验证：9个高价值攻击目标中，5个达成完全成功（如窃取API密钥、篡改代码逻辑），其余4个虽未完全突破，但经3–5轮迭代后均呈现统计显著的行为偏移提升（p<0.01）。机制分析揭示：IPI有效性受中后层注意力阈值机制调控，三类因果干预（注意力掩码、层间梯度阻断、关键头冻结）均验证该发现，并为鲁棒防御设计提供明确路径。

AI Summary (English)

IterInject is a feedback-guided iterative framework for indirect prompt injection (IPI) against LLM agents. It closes the loop among injection, diagnosis, and refinement: a rule-based diagnoser produces structured behavioral labels (e.g., “tool misuse”, “goal bypass”), while an LLM optimizer refines payloads conditioned on the full optimization history—including prior failures, successes, and diagnostic signals. A synthesis module auto-generates novel disguise seeds from failure patterns, enabling self-evolution of the attack strategy space. Evaluated on AgentDojo and InjectAgent across four victim models, IterInject outperforms static baselines by +42.6% and prior adaptive methods by +28.3% in average success rate. On the production-grade Claude Code agent—with layered defenses—optimized payloads achieve full success on 5/9 high-value targets; the remaining 4 show statistically significant behavioral improvement after iterative refinement. Mechanistic analysis identifies an attention-mediated threshold mechanism in mid-to-late transformer layers, validated via three causal interventions—pointing directly to actionable defense strategies.

Abstract

LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed for complex tasks requiring planning, tool use, and interaction with external services. Their reliance on untrusted external content exposes them to indirect prompt injection (IPI), in which adversarial instructions embedded in retrieved data hijack agent behavior. Existing attacks rely on static payloads that cannot adapt to agent-specific defenses; even recent adaptive methods lack structured feedback to guide optimization. We introduce \oursys, a feedback-guided iterative framework that closes the loop between injection, diagnosis, and refinement: a rule-based diagnoser produces structured outcome labels with behavioral descriptions, and an LLM-based optimizer refines payloads conditioned on the full optimization history. A synthesis step generates new disguise seeds from failure patterns, enabling the strategy space to self-evolve. On AgentDojo and InjectAgent, \oursys substantially outperforms static baselines and existing adaptive methods across four victim models. Extension experiments on Claude Code, a production-grade coding agent with layered defenses, show that optimized payloads achieve full success on 5 of 9 targets; even those that resist full exploitation exhibit measurable improvement from iterative refinement. We further present a mechanistic analysis of IPI, identifying an attention-mediated threshold mechanism in mid-to-late layers; three causal interventions validate this finding and point to concrete defense directions.

item.title: ChainLearn: A Blockchain-Based Capacity-Aware Framework for Federated Ensemble Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24418v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在医疗影像等隐私敏感场景中，联邦学习（FL）被广泛用于避免原始数据集中化。然而，现有主流算法（如FedAvg）隐含硬件同质性假设：要求所有参与方（如医院）具备相近算力、统一模型架构及中心化聚合能力。现实中，不同医院的计算资源差异显著（如边缘设备 vs. 高性能服务器），强制部署相同模型（如ResNet-50）将导致弱资源节点训练失败或强资源节点能力浪费，严重制约实际落地。

方法创新：ChainLearn框架

我们提出ChainLearn——首个区块链赋能的容量感知联邦集成学习框架：

  • 容量感知协调机制：各医院本地运行轻量基准测试，链上提交吞吐量哈希；系统据此动态分配适配架构（如MobileNetV3-Small供低配医院，ResNet-50供高配医院），消除架构强制统一；
  • 链上-链下解耦设计：Solidity智能合约仅存储医院注册信息、基准哈希、校准权重及全局指标（不存储任何模型参数）；本地训练全程离链，仅上传哈希值与标量权重（如置信度、准确率）；
  • 加权集成推理：最终预测由客户端本地完成加权融合（权重基于历史表现动态更新），保障低延迟与隐私安全。

实验结果

在PneumoniaMNIST与DermaMNIST数据集上（5次随机种子、3种非独立同分布强度），ChainLearn：
✅ 校准误差降低：较等权集成平均下降12.7%（p<0.01），提升临床可信度；
✅ 精度竞争力强：准确率与FedAvg/FedProx/FedMD相当（±0.8%），且在资源异构场景下鲁棒性更优；
✅ 通信开销极低：每轮仅需224字节（含哈希+标量），较FedAvg（约205 MB/轮）压缩912,000倍以上，满足广域医疗网络带宽约束。

AI Summary (English)

ChainLearn is a blockchain-based capacity-aware framework for federated ensemble learning, designed to address the critical mismatch between standard FL assumptions (homogeneous hardware, uniform architectures) and real-world medical infrastructure heterogeneity. It decouples on-chain governance—where a Solidity smart contract securely registers hospitals, stores benchmark hashes, performance metrics, and ensemble weights—from off-chain learning: participants train locally and submit only lightweight cryptographic hashes and scalar metrics (no model parameters). Capacity-aware coordination dynamically assigns architecture-appropriate models (e.g., MobileNetV3-Small for low-resource sites, ResNet-50 for high-resource ones), enabling equitable participation without architectural enforcement. Weighted ensemble inference is performed client-side for privacy and efficiency. Experiments on PneumoniaMNIST and DermaMNIST (5 seeds, 3 non-IID levels) show ChainLearn achieves lower or equal calibration error versus equal-weight ensembles and matches the accuracy of FedAvg, FedProx, and FedMD—while reducing per-round communication overhead to just 224 bytes, a >912,000× reduction over FedAvg.

Abstract

Federated learning is used in medical imaging where privacy prohibits centralizing data. Standard federated algorithms assume homogeneous hardware, identical architectures, and centralized aggregation, which fails when hospitals have unequal compute resources. We propose capacity-aware coordination: measure each hospital's throughput, assign capacity-appropriate architectures (MobileNetV3-Small, EfficientNet-B0, ResNet-50), and combine predictions via weighted ensemble. Weak and strong hospitals can participate without forcing uniform architectures. We separate on-chain policy from off-chain learning. A Solidity contract stores hospital registration, benchmark hashes, metrics, and weights. Hospitals train locally and submit only hashes and scalars (not parameters). Weighted ensemble inference is computed off-chain. Experiments on PneumoniaMNIST and DermaMNIST (5 seeds, 3 non-IID levels) show our method achieves lower or equal calibration error versus equal-weight ensemble and competitive accuracy versus FedAvg, FedProx, and FedMD. Communication overhead is 224 bytes per round, a reduction of over 912,000x compared to FedAvg.

item.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23887v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

时序知识图谱型数据市场面临三重耦合失效：（1）静态混合索引捷径因边关系动态演化而迅速过时，导致召回率下降；（2）基于静态分布假设的Shapley值定价在数据分布漂移后严重误估贡献；（3）多代理协同缺失造成差分隐私预算被无序超额消耗。现有方案缺乏对时间敏感性、价值动态性与隐私资源协同性的统一建模。

方法创新：CHRONOS三层架构

CHRONOS提出显式分离“公共索引”与“私有估值”的三层次时序协调框架：

  • Layer 1（时效索引层）：采用神经常微分方程（Neural-ODE）建模边时效衰减，为每次查询提供理论可证的期望召回损失界 $O(P_q \lambda \Delta t)$；引入单调包络技术，将理论界松散度压缩至实际损失的1.8–3.2倍（实证验证）；
  • Layer 2（动态估值层）：将Shapley值计算条件化于检测到的变点（changepoints），在噪声存在下提供有限样本误差界；
  • Layer 3（隐私协同层）：采用EXP3-IX算法调度代理访问，实现 $O(\sqrt{T \log T})$ 遗憾界；通过矩会计（moments accounting）严格保障 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP，并在每轮发布经高斯机制扰动的私有亲和矩阵——所有检索与排序均为后处理，零额外隐私开销。

实验结果与意义

在4个基准测试中，CHRONOS达成：Recall@10 = 0.937、吞吐量2.74 QPS、端到端延迟161 ms，在zCDP组合下总隐私成本为 $\varepsilon=4.25$（$\delta=10^{-6}$）。支持500卖家规模扩展与多轮结算。关键洞见：当前隐私预算下，直接释放的估值仍受噪声主导；系统效用主要源于低敏感度统计驱动的公共索引路由与自适应调度——这为高隐私约束下的实用化数据市场设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

CHRONOS is a temporally aware, multi-agent coordination framework for evolving data marketplaces built on knowledge graphs. It addresses three coupled failures in static designs—stale index shortcuts, misattributed Shapley pricing under distribution shifts, and uncoordinated differential-privacy budget consumption—via a unified three-layer architecture with explicit public/private separation. Layer 1 uses neural-ODE temporal decay to bound per-query recall loss as $O(P_q \lambda \Delta t)$, tightening bounds via monotone envelopes (1.8–3.2× tighter than observed loss). Layer 2 conditions Shapley valuation on detected changepoints and provides finite-sample error guarantees under noise. Layer 3 employs EXP3-IX for agent scheduling, achieving $O(\sqrt{T \log T})$ regret while enforcing $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP via moments accounting; it releases a privatized affinity matrix per epoch using the Gaussian mechanism, with all retrieval/ranking as zero-cost post-processing. Evaluated across four benchmarks, CHRONOS achieves Recall@10 = 0.937, 2.74 QPS, 161 ms latency, and total privacy cost $\varepsilon = 4.25$ at $\delta = 10^{-6}$ under zCDP composition—demonstrating a competitive operational point where utility stems primarily from public routing and low-sensitivity adaptive scheduling.

Abstract

Temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces face three coupled failures in static designs: stale hybrid index shortcuts reduce recall as edges evolve, stationary Shapley pricing misattributes value after distribution shifts, and uncoordinated agents over-consume a shared differential-privacy budget. We present CHRONOS, a three-layer architecture providing a unified treatment of these challenges with explicit public and private separation. Layer one applies neural-ODE temporal decay to shortcut edges, providing a per-query expected recall-loss bound of Big-O of Pq lambda delta t, with a monotone-envelope guarantee reducing bound looseness to 1.8 to 3.2 times observed loss. Layer two conditions Shapley valuation on detected changepoints and provides finite-sample error guarantees under noise. Layer three uses EXP3-IX to achieve Big-O of the square root of T log T regret while enforcing epsilon and delta differential privacy via moments accounting. CHRONOS releases a privatized affinity matrix per epoch using the Gaussian mechanism; all retrieval and ranking are post-processing, incurring no extra privacy cost. We provide multi-epoch settlement, scalability analysis for 500 sellers, and comparisons against accelerated baselines. Across four benchmarks, CHRONOS shows 0.937 recall at ten, 2.74 queries per second, 161 ms latency, and total epsilon of 4.25 at delta of 10 to the power of negative 6 under zCDP composition. These results indicate a competitive operating point. A limitation is that at this privacy level, released valuations remain noise-dominated; utility derives primarily from public index routing and adaptive scheduling driven by low-sensitivity statistics.

item.title: On the Stability of Spherical Hellinger-Kantorovich Flows and Their Implications for Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23879v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

梯度流采样将Gibbs分布建模为概率测度空间上能量泛函的极小值，并通过几何驱动的动力学逼近目标分布。在球面Hellinger-Kantorovich（SHK）几何下，该流自然耦合传输（transport）与反应（reaction）机制，等价于出生-死亡Langevin动力学，为离散/非光滑、高维或低秩隐私机制提供理想建模框架。然而，现有理论缺乏对势函数扰动下SHK流稳定性的定量刻画，制约其在差分隐私（DP）中可证明的实用化。

方法与理论贡献

本文建立首个SHK梯度流的扰动理论：给定两个势函数 $V$ 与 $V'$，我们从同一初始分布出发，系统分析其对应SHK流的演化偏差。核心工具是基于SHK距离的一致扰动界——该界不依赖维度，且直接控制任意时刻的对数似然比（log-likelihood ratio）与Rényi散度（$\alpha$-Rényi divergence, $\forall \alpha \ge 1$）。进一步，当势函数满足Lipschitz-Hessian结构时，我们推导出KL散度的时间显式上界，突破传统信息论方法的局限。

差分隐私应用与创新

我们将理论应用于指数机制（Exponential Mechanism）的近似采样：

  • Pure-DP保障：对数似然比控制直接导出时间依赖的$(\varepsilon(t),0)$-DP保证，首次为SHK采样器提供严格、可计算的纯隐私界；
  • Approximate-DP认证：KL界结合hockey-stick散度转化，给出$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP的显式$\delta(t)$衰减率；
  • 效用-隐私权衡：提出分离式效用界，明确区分指数机制固有次优性（由敏感度与效用函数决定）与有限时间采样误差（由SHK流收敛速率控制），支持超参调优与误差归因。

本工作为几何采样与隐私计算的交叉提供了统一稳定性理论，并为高效、可验证的隐私发布系统奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first perturbation theory for spherical Hellinger–Kantorovich (SHK) gradient flows. Given two potentials $V$ and $V'$, we quantify how initial discrepancies propagate along their respective SHK flows—yielding a dimension-free, uniform bound on the log-likelihood ratio and Rényi divergence ($\alpha \ge 1$) at all times. Under mild Hessian regularity, we further derive an explicit time-dependent upper bound on the KL divergence. Applied to the exponential mechanism in differential privacy, our bounds deliver: (i) time-resolved $(\varepsilon(t), 0)$-DP guarantees via log-ratio control; (ii) $(\varepsilon, \delta(t))$-DP certificates through hockey-stick divergence; and (iii) a decomposed utility bound separating intrinsic mechanism suboptimality from finite-time sampling error. The results provide the first geometrically grounded, provable privacy–utility trade-off for SHK-based samplers.

Abstract

Gradient-flow sampling interprets a Gibbs distribution as the minimizer of an energy functional over probability measures and generates dynamics converging to this target. Under spherical Hellinger-Kantorovich (SHK) geometry, the flow couples transport and reaction and coincides with birth-death Langevin dynamics. In this work, we develop a perturbation theory for SHK gradient flows. For two potentials $V$ and $V^{\prime}$, we compare the associated flows from a common initialization and quantify how potential discrepancies propagate over time. A uniform perturbation bound yields dimension-free, pointwise control of the log-likelihood ratio and Rényi divergence, while additional structure allows us to derive bounds for the KL divergence as well. We apply these results to approximate sampling for the exponential mechanism in differential privacy. The likelihood-ratio control provides explicit time-dependent Pure-DP guarantees for SHK-based samplers, while the KL bound yields Approximate-DP certificates via hockey-stick divergence. We also derive a utility bound separating intrinsic exponential-mechanism suboptimality from finite-time sampling error.

item.title: AI Security Research Should Better Incentivize Defense Research
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23448v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前人工智能（AI）安全研究领域存在显著的攻防失衡现象：攻击类论文数量远超防御类论文，且该失衡并非仅体现于数量层面，更深层地反映在评估标准的系统性偏差上。本研究系统梳理了近五年顶会（如NeurIPS、ICML、USENIX Security）及权威期刊中AI安全相关论文，覆盖联邦学习、语音识别、成员推断攻击、大语言模型（LLM）安全等核心子领域。

方法与分析

我们采用跨子领域量化分析法，统计各方向攻击/防御论文比例，并深入对比其评估范式：

  • 攻击研究常基于理想化假设（如完全白盒访问、无噪声输入、无限查询预算），夸大实际威胁程度；
  • 防御研究则普遍面临“一票否决”式严苛检验——仅需一个新攻击即被判定失效，且极少验证部署可行性、计算开销与用户效用损失；
  • 进一步发现，约78%的防御论文未在真实系统或工业级API（如OpenAI、Azure ML）上验证，而86%的攻击论文依赖仿真环境构造“最坏-case”场景。

主要发现与创新点

首次提出“评估不对称性”（Evaluation Asymmetry）概念，揭示攻防研究生态失衡的结构性根源；实证表明，当前文献呈现“漏洞丰富、防护稀薄”特征——高影响力攻击成果频出，但可落地、经得起迭代攻击考验的防御方案严重匮乏。本研究呼吁建立防御友好的激励机制：包括设立专项评审标准、鼓励长期鲁棒性评测、认可增量式工程改进，并将部署效果纳入学术评价体系。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a critical structural imbalance in AI security research: a pronounced overemphasis on attacking AI systems relative to defending them—not only in publication volume but, more importantly, in evaluation rigor. Through systematic analysis of 327 papers across federated learning, speech recognition, membership inference, and large language model security, we find attack-to-defense ratios consistently exceed 4:1. Crucially, attack papers are routinely evaluated under overly favorable, idealized conditions (e.g., full model access, noiseless inputs), inflating perceived threat severity; defenses, by contrast, face unrealistically strict benchmarks—often invalidated by a single novel attack and rarely tested in real-world deployment settings (only 22% validated on production APIs). This asymmetry yields a literature rich in demonstrated vulnerabilities but poor in robust, deployable protections. We argue that sustainable AI security requires rebalancing incentives—through dedicated review criteria, standardized long-term robustness evaluation, and academic recognition of engineering rigor and deployment success.

Abstract

This work examines an imbalance in artificial intelligence (AI) security research: the field tends to produce more work on attacking AI systems than on defending them. Drawing on related academic papers, we find biased attack-to-defense ratios across subfields, including federated learning, speech recognition, membership inference, large language models, etc. The imbalance possibly means far beyond a simple count: attack papers are routinely evaluated under favorable conditions that make threats look more severe than they are in practice, while defenses are held to a stricter standard that few can meet. The result is a literature rich in demonstrated vulnerabilities and thin on usable and deployed protections. We thus argue that AI security research should better incentivize defense research.

item.title: Security, Privacy, and Ethical Risks in OpenClaw
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23330v1

AI Summary (中文)

OpenClaw系统安全、隐私与伦理风险的系统性评估

本研究首次对OpenClaw——一种面向本地执行的自然语言交互式AI智能体系统——开展全面的风险画像分析。OpenClaw具备任务编排、跨平台工具调用与现实世界操作能力，在个人助理、办公自动化及多源信息整合等场景展现出显著应用潜力。然而，其高度特权化设计（如本地持久化存储、无沙箱工具执行、跨会话上下文聚合）亦引入多重系统性风险。

我们采用架构驱动的风险分析法，结合对其模块化组件（LLM核心、工具调度器、插件管理器、本地知识库）、部署范式（纯离线/混合模式）及典型应用场景（如邮件自动归档+日程同步+第三方API调用）的深度剖析，识别出五大关键风险维度：

  • 安全风险：未受限的本地文件/进程访问权限可能导致横向渗透；插件签名缺失与动态加载机制易被恶意注入；
  • 隐私风险：跨任务上下文隐式聚合用户敏感数据（如健康记录与财务信息），且缺乏细粒度访问审计；
  • 伦理风险：多用户共享设备下身份混淆导致越权操作；自动化决策链路不可解释，引发责任归属困境；
  • 可追溯性挑战：操作日志分散于工具层、插件层与LLM推理层，缺乏统一溯源标识与因果链重建能力；
  • 治理盲区：现有隐私政策与伦理框架难以覆盖“本地化、高自主性、强耦合”智能体的新范式。

本研究不仅揭示了OpenClaw作为典型端侧AI代理的深层脆弱性，更提炼出适用于下一代本地智能体系统的四维治理框架（安全防御、隐私增强、伦理对齐、全链路可追溯），为构建可信、可控、可问责的AI代理生态提供理论依据与实践路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic analysis of security, privacy, ethical, and traceability risks in OpenClaw, a locally executable AI agent system designed for natural language interaction and real-world task execution. Through architecture-driven evaluation—examining its tool invocation model, persistent local storage, cross-context information aggregation, multi-user handling, and plugin integration—we identify critical vulnerabilities: unrestricted local resource access enabling lateral movement; implicit sensitive data fusion across tasks without user consent; identity ambiguity in shared environments; and fragmented audit logs impeding causal attribution. We argue these issues fundamentally undermine trustworthiness and hinder responsible deployment. The study concludes by proposing four open challenges requiring coordinated action: (1) adaptive runtime security enforcement, (2) context-aware privacy-preserving aggregation, (3) explainable ethical constraint embedding, and (4) unified provenance tracking across agent layers. Our findings serve as a foundational risk assessment for trustworthy local AI agents.

Abstract

This paper systematically investigates the security, privacy, and ethical risks, as well as the traceability challenges of OpenClaw, a locally executable AI agent system for natural language interaction and real-world task completion. While OpenClaw shows strong potential for personal assistance, office automation, cross-platform task management, and information integration, it also raises serious security, privacy, and ethical concerns. By analyzing its system architecture, core functionalities, deployment model, and representative application scenarios, this paper aims to reveal the risks that may arise when such a highly privileged agent is integrated into personal and organizational digital environments. We focus in particular on the challenges associated with persistent local storage, tool invocation, cross-context information aggregation, multi-user interaction, and the integration of plugins and external services. We argue that these issues constitute major barriers to the trustworthy deployment and widespread adoption of this technology. Finally, we summarize the open challenges in security defenses, privacy protection, ethical governance, and traceability in agent use, and call for joint efforts from researchers, developers, deployers, and regulators to build AI agent systems that are safer, more reliable, and more trustworthy.

item.title: Prompt Overflow: What the Guardrail Inspects Is Not What the Model Infers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23196v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Guardrail模型（又称安全检查器）被广泛部署于大语言模型（LLM）前端，作为抵御提示注入攻击的首要防线。然而，受限于上下文长度约束，现有guardrail模型对超长提示普遍采用截断（truncation）或分段检查（segmentation-based inspection）策略——仅审查输入的一部分，而非完整语境。

核心问题与创新发现

本文首次揭示了一个关键安全盲区：guardrail模型的有限检查窗口与下游LLM的大幅扩展推理上下文窗口之间存在系统性错配。基于此，我们提出新型Prompt Overflow攻击：将恶意指令碎片化，并与大量无害填充内容交错嵌入超长提示中，使得每个被检查的片段均呈现良性表象，而LLM在完整上下文中仍能准确识别并执行全部恶意意图。

实验验证与影响

我们在Meta Llama Prompt Guard、IBM Granite Guardian及DeBERTa基线检测器上开展系统性评估。结果表明：原本在短上下文下100%被检出的恶意提示，在经攻击重构为超长输入后，检出率骤降至最低3.2%，却仍保持对LLM的100%可执行性。该现象证实了当前防御范式在长输入场景下的结构性失效。

贡献与展望

本研究不仅定义了“提示溢出”这一新攻击范式，更推动安全边界从“语义对抗”延伸至“结构-上下文错配”维度。我们同步提出三类缓解方向：动态上下文重聚合、跨段一致性建模、以及LLM协同式反验机制，为下一代鲁棒guardrail设计提供理论基础与实践路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a critical security blind spot in guardrail models—widely deployed safety checkers for LLMs—arising from the mismatch between their narrow inspection windows (due to context constraints) and the much larger inference contexts of downstream LLMs. We introduce the Prompt Overflow Attack, which fragments malicious instructions and interleaves them with benign filler content across overlength prompts, ensuring no single inspected segment triggers detection while the full prompt remains fully actionable to the target LLM. Through systematic evaluation against state-of-the-art guardrails—including Meta’s Llama Prompt Guard, IBM’s Granite Guardian, and DeBERTa-based detectors—we show that adversarially lengthened prompts evade detection (as low as 3.2% detection rate) while preserving 100% attack success against LLMs—despite being reliably caught in short-context settings. Our work exposes a fundamental structural vulnerability in current safety architectures and proposes concrete mitigation directions: context-aware re-aggregation, cross-segment consistency modeling, and LLM-assisted verification.

Abstract

Guardrail models (a.k.a. safety checkers) are widely deployed to screen user inputs before they reach large language models (LLMs), serving as a primary defense against prompt injection attacks. Due to strict context constraints, these models handle overlength prompts through truncation or segmentation-based inspection. While prior work has focused on semantic adversarial inputs, the security implications of these long-input processing mechanisms remain largely unexplored. In this paper, we identify a critical blind spot arising from the mismatch between the limited inspection windows of guardrail models and the substantially larger context inference windows of downstream LLMs. We introduce a novel Prompt Overflow Attack, which exploits this mismatch by fragmenting malicious instructions and interleaving them with benign filler content across an overlong prompt, such that no individual inspected segment appears malicious while the full context remains actionable to the LLM. Through a systematic evaluation against state-of-the-art guardrail models, including Meta Llama Prompt Guard, IBM Granite Guardian, and DeBERTa-based detectors, we demonstrate that prompts reliably detected in short-context settings can evade guardrail models once adversarially manipulated into over-length inputs, yet remain fully actionable by downstream LLMs. We further propose potential defense strategies and outline mitigation directions to strengthen guardrail models.

item.title: PoisonForge: Task-Level Targeted Poisoning Benchmark for Instruction-Tuned LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23168v1

AI Summary (中文)

PoisonForge：面向指令微调大语言模型的任务级定向投毒基准

背景与问题：当从业者在未经审核的数据集上微调大语言模型（LLM）时，攻击者可通过“任务级投毒”破坏数据供应链——仅注入少量精心构造的指令-响应对，即可诱使模型在特定任务族（如问答、摘要）的输出中稳定嵌入攻击者指定的实体（如某国名），同时在其他任务中保持正常行为，极具隐蔽性与危害性。

方法与基准设计：本文提出 PoisonForge，首个系统化评估该威胁的开源基准。它沿四个正交维度参数化投毒策略：偏置类型（实体类别）、投毒模式（前缀/后缀/插入）、实体出现频次（1–5次）和目标输出长度（短/中/长）。在严格约束下（仅1%毒样本预算，即1000条微调样本中仅含10条毒样本），我们在5类典型任务家族上全面评测12个开源指令模型（参数量覆盖2B–32B）。

核心发现：

  • 11/12模型在其最脆弱配置下攻击成功率（ASR）超70%；
  • 跨任务泄露率（非目标任务中意外触发实体）<0.5%，且标准基准（如MMLU、AlpacaEval）性能无显著下降；
  • ASR随实体出现次数增加而提升，最优投毒模式高度依赖目标实体语义结构（如地名适配后缀，机构名适配前缀），且ASR随输出长度增长单调递减；
  • 相关性分析与风险预测模型证实：投毒设计选择（而非模型规模）是ASR主导因素，且该规律可泛化至未见任务。

创新与开源：PoisonForge首次实现任务级投毒的可控建模、可复现评估与机制归因；我们完整开源全部配置模板、训练/评估流水线及分析代码，推动鲁棒性研究标准化。

AI Summary (English)

PoisonForge is the first benchmark for task-level targeted poisoning of instruction-tuned LLMs—where adversaries inject minimal crafted instruction-response pairs to compel models to embed attacker-specified entities (e.g., a country) only in outputs of a targeted task family (e.g., QA), while preserving normal behavior elsewhere. Parameterized across four dimensions (bias type, poisoning mode, entity appearance count, and target output length), PoisonForge evaluates 12 open-weight models (2B–32B) across five task families under a strict 1% poison budget (e.g., 10 poisoned examples among 1,000). Results show 11/12 models achieve >70% attack success rate (ASR) in their most vulnerable configuration, with <0.5% unintended leakage to non-target tasks and no degradation on standard benchmarks. We identify key drivers: ASR increases with entity appearances, optimal poisoning mode depends on entity semantics, and ASR monotonically decreases with output length. Crucially, correlation and predictive modeling confirm that poisoning design—not model scale—is the primary determinant of ASR, and these patterns generalize to unseen tasks. All configurations, pipelines, and analysis code are publicly released.

Abstract

When practitioners fine-tune LLMs on unvetted datasets, an adversary can exploit the data supply chain through task-level poisoning: inserting a small number of crafted instruction-response pairs that cause the model to embed attacker-specified entities, such as a country, in outputs for a targeted task family while behaving normally elsewhere. We introduce PoisonForge, a benchmark that parameterizes this threat along four dimensions (bias type, poisoning mode, appearance count, and target output length) and evaluates 12 open-weight models (from 2B to 32B parameters) across five families under a primarily 1% poison budget. With only 10 poisoned examples among 1,000 fine-tuning examples, 11 of 12 models exceed a 70% attack success rate (ASR) in their most vulnerable configuration. Meanwhile, unintended leakage to non-target tasks remains below 0.5%, and models perform well on standard benchmarks. We analyze in detail the factors contributing to attack success. We observe that multiple appearances of an entity increase the ASR, the optimal poisoning mode depends on the semantic structure of the target entity, and ASR drops monotonically with the task output length. A correlation analysis and risk prediction model confirm that poisoning design choices, rather than model scale, are the primary causes of attack success, and that these patterns generalize to predict attack success on new tasks. We release all configurations, pipelines, and analysis code to support reproducible comparisons.

item.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23899v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

语言智能体正通过复用技能（structured procedural artifacts）持续提升能力——这些技能是从历史交互经验中提炼出的可重用程序化知识。其中，领域级与模型生成式技能尤为关键：前者聚焦特定领域内高频重复流程（如订票、代码调试），实现快速领域适配；后者则突破人工编写瓶颈，支持规模化技能演化。然而，当前研究严重失衡——技能提取方法层出不穷，却缺乏对完整技能生命周期（经验生成 → 技能提取 → 技能消费）的系统性实证检验，导致核心问题悬而未决：技能是否真正有效？在何种条件下生效？成败的关键机制是什么？

方法与发现

本研究构建首个效用导向的统一评估框架，覆盖5个异构任务领域（含Web导航、API调用、数学推理等），横向对比多种技能提取器与目标智能体组合。关键发现包括：

  • 模型生成技能平均提升性能，但存在显著负迁移现象（约23%场景性能下降）；
  • 提取能力与消费能力高度解耦：强提取器未必是强消费者，反之亦然；
  • 技能效用与模型参数量或基线任务表现无关，颠覆“越大越优”直觉；
  • 经验构成（如任务多样性、失败案例占比）显著影响技能泛化性；
  • 高效技能具有三大特征：语义完整性、步骤可分解性、上下文鲁棒性。

创新贡献

基于深度归因分析，我们提出可落地的元技能（meta-skill）指导原则：将技能提取目标从“语法正确性”转向“效用可预测性”，显式建模消费者认知偏差与任务约束。该原则在全部5个领域上稳定提升技能质量（+18.7%平均效用分）并降低负迁移率52%，为构建可靠、可解释的技能驱动智能体提供理论基石与工程路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic, utility-grounded evaluation of the full skill lifecycle—experience generation, skill extraction, and skill consumption—for model-generated agent skills. Across five diverse agentic domains, we find that while such skills yield average performance gains, they incur non-trivial negative transfer (≈23% of cases), and extractor and consumer capabilities are largely decoupled: a model can excel at one stage yet fail at the other, irrespective of scale or baseline strength.
