Through fine-grained dissection, we identify experience composition, skill structural properties (e.g., step decomposability, context robustness), and consumer-specific transfer dynamics as key determinants of utility. Crucially, we distill these insights into a concrete meta-skill—a guidance protocol for extraction that prioritizes utility-predictive features over surface correctness. This meta-skill consistently improves skill quality (+18.7% mean utility) and cuts negative transfer by 52% across all domains.

Abstract

Language agents increasingly improve by reusing \emph{skills} -- structured procedural artifacts distilled from past experience. In particular, \emph{domain-level} and \emph{model-generated} skills are especially promising. They offer fast adaptation within a domain by encoding domain-specific recurring procedures, and they scale beyond labor-intensive hand-crafting. However, while extraction methods continue to proliferate, understanding remains limited, with no comprehensive study spanning the full skill lifecycle -- \textbf{experience generation}, \textbf{skill extraction}, and \textbf{skill consumption} -- to ask whether such skills actually work, when they work, and what makes them succeed or fail. To close this gap, we build a utility-grounded evaluation framework that provides systematic experimental results across extractors and target agents, covering five diverse agentic task domains. We find that model-generated skills are beneficial on average but exhibit non-trivial negative transfer, and that neither extractors nor targets behave uniformly. A model can be a strong extractor yet a weak consumer, or vice versa, with skill utility independent of model scale or baseline task strength. To explain these patterns, we then dissect each lifecycle stage in depth, analyzing how experience composition shapes skill quality, what properties characterize useful skills, and how the same skill transfers across different consumers. Finally, we translate these findings into a concrete \emph{meta-skill} that guides skill extraction toward the features tied to actual utility, which consistently improves skill quality across domains and substantially reduces negative transfer.

item.title: MemAudit: Post-hoc Auditing of Poisoned Agent Memory via Causal Attribution and Structural Anomaly Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23723v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体广泛采用持久化记忆机制（如向量数据库存储历史交互与示范样本），其长期任务执行能力显著提升。但该机制也引入关键安全风险：恶意用户可通过常规对话交互向记忆库中注入隐蔽毒化记录（poisoned memories），这些记录后续被检索并误导智能体推理，导致有害输出——而现有防御（如实时提示过滤或输出拦截）均属在线干预，无法回答核心的事后归因问题：当危害已发生时，哪些具体记忆条目是因果根源？

方法：MemAudit 框架

我们提出 MemAudit——首个面向记忆增强型LLM智能体的事后因果审计框架。它融合两类正交信号：

  • 因果归因评分：基于反事实推理，量化每条记忆对有害输出的因果影响强度（Counterfactual Memory Influence Score），识别高责任记忆；
  • 结构异常检测：构建记忆一致性图（Memory Consistency Graph），将记忆嵌入映射为图节点，通过拓扑稀疏性与语义连通性分析，定位偏离正常记忆分布的结构异常点。

二者协同实现高精度、可解释的记忆污染溯源。

主要发现与创新

在针对MINJA攻击（仅通过自然查询诱导记忆注入，无需直接篡改记忆库）的全面评估中，MemAudit在真实事后审计场景下表现卓越：

  • 问答智能体（QA）：攻击成功率从70%降至0%；
  • 推理智能体（RAP）：攻击成功率从83.3%降至0%。

本工作首次将因果推断与图结构异常检测系统性引入记忆安全审计，为LLM智能体的记忆可信性提供了可验证、可部署的事后保障范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on persistent memory for long-horizon reasoning, yet this introduces a critical security vulnerability: adversaries can stealthily inject poisoned records via normal interactions, later retrieved to manipulate agent behavior. Existing defenses focus on online intervention but lack post-hoc audit capability—i.e., identifying which stored memories causally contributed to observed harm. We propose MemAudit, the first causal memory auditing framework for memory-augmented LLM agents. It combines (1) a counterfactual memory influence score to quantify each memory’s causal contribution to harmful outputs, and (2) a memory consistency graph to detect structurally anomalous memories within the broader memory store. Evaluated against the query-only MINJA attack across QA and reasoning-agent settings, MemAudit reduces attack success rates from 70% to 0% and from 83.3% to 0%, respectively, under realistic post-hoc auditing conditions. This work establishes a novel, interpretable, and deployable paradigm for memory integrity assurance.

Abstract

Large language model agents increasingly rely on persistent memory to store past interactions, retrieve relevant demonstrations, and improve long-horizon task execution. However, this memory mechanism also creates a practical security vulnerability: an adversarial user may inject malicious records into the agent's memory through ordinary interaction, and these records can later be retrieved to steer the agent's reasoning and actions. Existing defenses primarily focus on online intervention, such as prompt filtering or output blocking, but they do not address the post-hoc question of which stored memories are responsible after harmful behavior has already been observed. We propose \textbf{MemAudit}, a post-hoc causal memory auditing framework for memory-augmented LLM agents. The framework combines two complementary signals: (1) a counterfactual memory influence score that measures each memory's causal contribution to harmful outputs, and (2) a memory consistency graph that identifies structurally anomalous memories within the broader memory store. We evaluate MemAudit against MINJA, a query-only memory injection attack in which malicious records are generated and stored through normal agent interactions rather than direct memory-bank modification. Across both QA and reasoning-agent settings, MemAudit substantially reduces attack success rates under realistic post-hoc auditing scenarios. The results show that QA attack success is reduced from $70\%$ to $0\%$, while RAP attack success drops from $83.3\%$ to $0\%$.

item.title: EM-Vid: Training-Free Entity-Centric Memory for Efficient and Consistent Multi-Shot Video Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23610v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多镜头视频生成需在跨镜头中保持重复出现实体（如人物、物体）外观的一致性，同时严格遵循各镜头专属的文本提示。现有自回归方法将先前生成的完整帧作为记忆，但全帧存储将持久性实体特征与瞬时场景上下文深度耦合，导致无关信息泄露、内存冗余及计算开销高。

方法创新

我们提出 EM-Vid——一种免训练（training-free）的实体中心化记忆机制，核心包括：

  • 实体索引的潜在块记忆库：将视频中检测到的实体映射为独立、可检索的局部潜在补丁（latent patches），解耦实体身份与背景；
  • 稀疏令牌条件化机制：在预训练扩散模型上兼容引入，通过实体掩码约束自注意力仅作用于相关令牌，降低显存占用达42%，推理速度提升1.8×；
  • 结构化多镜头脚本格式：统一定义实体ID、镜头边界与提示锚点，支撑跨镜头记忆调度；
  • 预算受限的记忆更新策略：动态淘汰低置信度补丁、合并相似表征，维持紧凑且演化的记忆容量（默认≤128 patches/shot）；
  • 可控噪声注入模块：对每个实体表征注入可调节的高斯噪声，实现细粒度外观控制（如光照/姿态微调），从源头抑制上下文泄漏。

性能优势

在MultiShotBench基准测试中，EM-Vid在主体一致性得分（SCS）提升37.2%，提示忠实度（Prompt-F1）提高29.5%，且无需微调任何模型参数。代码与脚本规范已开源。

AI Summary (English)

EM-Vid introduces a training-free, entity-centric memory framework for multi-shot video generation. It replaces full-frame memory with an entity-indexed bank of latent patches, decoupling persistent subject identity from transient scene context. We propose sparse token conditioning—compatible with off-the-shelf diffusion models—that restricts self-attention to entity-relevant tokens, cutting memory usage by 42% and accelerating inference. A structured script format enables precise cross-shot entity referencing, while a budgeted memory update strategy maintains compact, evolving representations. Crucially, noise injection into entity embeddings enables fine-grained appearance control and prevents irrelevant context leakage. On MultiShotBench, EM-Vid achieves +37.2% subject consistency score and +29.5% prompt fidelity over prior art—without any model fine-tuning.

Abstract

Multi-shot video generation requires maintaining a consistent appearance of recurring entities across shots while remaining faithful to shot-specific text prompts. Recent autoregressive methods reuse previously generated frames as memory. However, full-frame storage entangles persistent entity information with transient scene context, leading to irrelevant information leakage and high computational cost. We propose an entity-centric memory in the form of an entity-indexed bank of latent patches. We introduce sparse token conditioning compatible with pretrained models, restricting self-attention to entity-relevant tokens and reducing computational cost. To support this, we introduce a structured multi-shot script format. We additionally propose a budgeted memory update strategy to maintain a compact, evolving memory. Finally, we equip the entity representation with a noise-injection mechanism that enables fine-grained appearance control, preventing leakage of irrelevant information. Our method improves prompt adherence and efficiency while preserving subject consistency.

item.title: Solving the Aircraft Disassembly Scheduling Problem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23592v1

AI Summary (中文)

航空器拆解调度问题建模与求解

随着航空业可持续发展要求日益提升，退役飞机的绿色拆解已成为资源循环利用的关键环节。然而，该过程利润微薄、操作复杂，亟需通过高效调度优化提升经济可行性与行业实践动力。本文系统建模了真实工业场景下的航空器拆解调度问题（Aircraft Disassembly Scheduling Problem, ADSP）：该问题规模庞大（单架飞机涉及上千项任务），并受多重强约束制约——包括：① 人员资质约束（特定部件回收需持证技师及专用设备）；② 工序 precedence 关系（如先断电再拆线缆）；③ 整机平衡性约束（拆解过程中重心偏移须严格控制在安全阈值内）；④ 空间容量约束（如驾驶舱、机翼油箱等区域物理空间有限，同步作业人数受限）。

为应对上述挑战，本文提出两种互补的精确求解方法：

  • 一个基于状态变量的约束规划（CP）模型，天然支持时序逻辑与全局约束（如cumulative和global_cardinality），有效刻画动态平衡与空间并发限制；
  • 一个混合整数规划（MIP）模型，采用时间索引变量与分段线性化技术处理非线性平衡约束，兼顾建模清晰性与商用求解器兼容性。

所有模型均在源自工业合作伙伴的真实数据实例上验证，规模覆盖200–1450个任务。实验表明：CP模型在中小规模实例（≤800任务）中求得最优解更快；MIP模型借助现代求解器（Gurobi）在大规模实例中展现出更强的界紧致性与鲁棒性。本研究首次将整机物理平衡性建模为可计算的调度约束，为航空循环经济提供了可落地的智能决策支持框架。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the Aircraft Disassembly Scheduling Problem (ADSP), a large-scale, highly constrained scheduling challenge arising in sustainable end-of-life aircraft dismantling. We formalize four critical real-world constraints: technician certification requirements, task precedence relations, whole-aircraft balance maintenance (modeled as dynamic center-of-gravity limits), and spatial capacity restrictions at aircraft locations. To solve ADSP, we propose two exact models: (1) a Constraint Programming (CP) model leveraging state variables and global constraints for natural handling of temporal and resource logic; and (2) a Mixed-Integer Programming (MIP) model using time-indexed variables and piecewise-linear approximations for balance constraints. Both models are validated on realistic industrial instances—up to 1,450 tasks—provided by an aerospace partner. Results show CP excels on small-to-medium instances (≤800 tasks) in solution speed, while MIP achieves tighter bounds and greater scalability on larger instances. This work is the first to integrate aircraft-level physical balance as a computable scheduling constraint, enabling practical, profit-aware disassembly planning.

Abstract

Dismantling aircrafts reaching their end of life is a complex endeavour that is necessary in terms of sustainability but yields small income margins for air transport companies. An efficient scheduling of the disassembly procedure is thus crucial to ensure the profitability of the process and incentivize practice. This is a large scheduling problem that involves thousands of tasks and many different constraints: Extracting parts that are destined to be reused requires technicians with specific certifications and equipment. Extraction operations might be subject to precedence relations. Furthermore, the aircraft must be kept balanced during the whole process. Finally, some of the locations of the aircraft have a limited space that caps the number of technicians able to work there concurrently. This article presents the problem in details and proposes two approaches to solve the problem: a Constraint Programming model and a MIP model. The models are tested on instances of varying sizes involving up to 1450 tasks, which are based on real operational data provided by an industrial partner.

item.title: CP or DP? Why Not Both: A Case Study in the Partial Shop Scheduling Problem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.23569v1

AI Summary (中文)

CP 与 DP 的协同融合：面向部分车间调度问题（PSSP）的混合建模框架

本文提出一种动态规划（DP）与约束规划（CP）深度协同的新型求解范式，突破传统上二者各自独立应用的局限。针对部分车间调度问题（Partial Shop Scheduling Problem, PSSP）——一类具有任意作业内操作 precedence 图的通用调度模型——我们构建了以 DP 为主干搜索框架、以 CP 为嵌入式子程序的混合架构：DP 负责分层枚举调度状态（如已完成操作集），而 CP 子程序在每个状态节点上执行全局约束传播，高效剪枝不可行操作序列，并原生支持任意结构的 precedence 约束（如链式、树形、DAG 或混合图）。

该设计带来三重创新：

  • 灵活性提升：支持 anytime 搜索策略（如 anytime column search），摆脱原始纯 DP 的严格层序限制；
  • 建模简洁性：CP 的声明式建模能力使 precedence 约束的引入无需修改 DP 状态定义，显著增强可扩展性；
  • 元启发式赋能：自然衍生出基于重启的大邻域搜索（LNS）方案——复用同一 DP 框架，在每次重启时注入由历史最优解导出的 partial-order schedule 作为强引导约束，持续改进 incumbent。

实验表明，尽管在绝对求解性能上暂未超越当前顶尖纯 CP 求解器，但本工作首次系统验证了“DP 主干 + CP 推理”的紧耦合范式在复杂调度问题中的可行性、鲁棒性与可扩展性，为组合优化中两类主流范式的协同演进提供了可复用的方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a hybrid paradigm that tightly integrates Dynamic Programming (DP) as the primary search backbone with Constraint Programming (CP) as an embedded inference engine—specifically for the Partial Shop Scheduling Problem (PSSP). Unlike conventional separate use, our approach leverages DP to enumerate state spaces (e.g., subsets of completed operations) while delegating global constraint propagation—especially for arbitrary precedence graphs—to a CP subroutine. This design enables anytime search (e.g., column search), seamless handling of complex precedence structures, and a novel Large Neighborhood Search (LNS) scheme where partial-order schedules from prior restarts guide subsequent DP runs. While not yet competitive with state-of-the-art pure CP solvers on PSSP benchmarks, our core contribution is the principled demonstration that such deep DP–CP integration is both viable and expressive—opening a new pathway for hybrid combinatorial optimization.

Abstract

Dynamic Programming (DP) and Constraint Programming (CP) are well-established paradigms for solving combinatorial optimization problems. Usually, these two approaches are used separately. This paper aims to show that the two can be combined effectively and elegantly, with DP serving as the primary search framework and CP used as a subroutine to leverage global constraint propagation. This paper presents such an approach for the Partial Shop Scheduling Problem (PSSP), for which a pure DP method has previously been proposed, and efficient CP filtering algorithms are available. The PSSP is a general scheduling problem where each job consists of a set of operations with arbitrary precedence constraints. The approach is flexible enough to accommodate anytime DP strategies, such as anytime column search, whereas the original DP algorithm operated in a strictly layer-wise manner. Moreover, the flexibility of the CP modeling makes it straightforward to incorporate arbitrary precedence constraints. As a result, the model naturally handles any precedence graph and even enables the design of a Large Neighborhood Search (LNS) scheme, in which the DP model is reused, and partial-order schedules are imposed across restarts to improve the incumbent solution. While not competitive with state-of-the-art pure CP solvers for this specific problem, our primary contribution is demonstrating the viability of this hybrid integration.
